---
title: |
  Internet Appendix for: Your Paper Title \\
  \textit{\normalsize Preliminary draft --- please do not cite without permission}
author:
  - name: Your Name
    email: you@example.org
    affiliations:
      - name: Your Department, Your University
        address: Some Address, Country
        corresponding: true
  - name: Co-Author One
    email: one@example.org
    affiliations:
      - name: Co-Author Department, Co-Author University
        address: Some Address, Country
  - name: Co-Author Two
    email: two@example.org
    affiliations:
      - name: Co-Author Department, Co-Author University
        address: Some Address, Country
date: today
date-format: "[This revision:] D MMMM YYYY"
abstract: |
  This Internet Appendix provides supplementary results, robustness
  checks, additional tables and figures, and proofs that complement
  the main paper. Replace this paragraph with a one- or two-sentence
  description of what readers will find here.
bibliography: references-appendix.bib

format:
  pdf:
    pdf-engine: xelatex
    documentclass: article
    classoption:
      - "12pt"
    geometry:
      - margin=1in
    linestretch: 2
    indent: true
    fontsize: 12pt
    colorlinks: true
    linkcolor: red
    citecolor: blue
    urlcolor: magenta
    cite-method: natbib
    biblio-style: apalike
    number-sections: true
    keep-tex: true
    subparagraph: true
    include-in-header:
      text: |
        \usepackage{amsthm}
        \usepackage{subcaption}
        \usepackage{setspace}
        \usepackage{enumitem}
        \usepackage{multirow}
        \usepackage{longtable}
        \usepackage{tabularx}
        \usepackage{float}
        \usepackage{sectsty}

        \AtBeginEnvironment{tabular}{\onehalfspacing}
        \subsectionfont{\large\itshape}
        \subsubsectionfont{\large\itshape}

        \makeatletter
        \def\@seccntformat#1{\@ifundefined{#1@cntformat}%
            {\csname the#1\endcsname\quad}%
            {\csname #1@cntformat\endcsname}%
        }
        \def\section@cntformat{\thesection.\quad}
        \def\subsection@cntformat{\thesubsection \quad}
        \makeatother

        \tolerance=1
        \emergencystretch=\maxdimen
        \hyphenpenalty=10000
        \hbadness=10000

        \renewcommand{\thesection}{Appendix \Alph{section}}
        \renewcommand{\thesubsection}{\Alph{section}.\arabic{subsection}}
        \renewcommand{\thetable}{\Alph{section}.\arabic{table}}
        \renewcommand{\thefigure}{\Alph{section}.\arabic{figure}}
        \renewcommand{\theequation}{\Alph{section}.\arabic{equation}}

        \theoremstyle{plain}
        \newtheorem{theorem}{Theorem}[section]
        \newtheorem{proposition}[theorem]{Proposition}
        \newtheorem{lemma}[theorem]{Lemma}
        \theoremstyle{definition}
        \newtheorem{definition}[theorem]{Definition}
        \newtheorem{assumption}[theorem]{Assumption}
---

# Sample Construction and Matching {#sec-app-matching}

This appendix documents how the analysis sample was constructed.
The procedure consists of three steps: (i) merging the source
datasets on a common unit identifier, (ii) restricting to units
observed for at least 10 consecutive periods, and (iii) trimming
the most extreme 1% of observations on the focal regressor. The
final panel contains 1{,}000 units and 20 time periods.

## Matching Procedure {#sec-app-matching-procedure}

We match treated units to control units on a set of pre-period
covariates using nearest-neighbour matching with replacement. The
matched control sample is used in the robustness checks reported
in @sec-app-robust below.

# Auxiliary Methodology {#sec-app-method}

This appendix walks through derivations referenced in the main
text. Consider the within transformation that absorbs the unit
fixed effect:

$$
\tilde{y}_{it} = y_{it} - \bar{y}_i, \qquad
\tilde{x}_{it} = x_{it} - \bar{x}_i
$$ {#eq-app-within}

The within estimator $\hat{\beta}_{FE}$ is the OLS slope from
regressing $\tilde{y}_{it}$ on $\tilde{x}_{it}$ and analogously
demeaned controls:

$$
\widehat{\beta}_{FE} = \left( \widetilde{X}' \widetilde{X} \right)^{-1} \widetilde{X}' \widetilde{y}
$$ {#eq-app-baseline}

Under the assumption stated in the main text, $\hat{\beta}_{FE}$
is consistent for the parameter of interest. See @sec-app-proofs
for the formal argument.

# Robustness Tables {#sec-app-robust}

This appendix reports robustness checks that supplement the main
text. The headline finding survives: trimming, alternative
sub-samples, and unit-specific linear trends each leave the
point estimate within one standard error of the baseline of
$0.12$ reported in the main paper.

# Proofs {#sec-app-proofs}

::: {.proof}
*(Proof of the proposition in the main text.)* Under the assumption
that $\varepsilon_{it}$ is mean-zero and orthogonal to $x_{it}$
conditional on $\alpha_i$, within-unit demeaning removes $\alpha_i$
from the estimating equation. The transformed regressor
$\tilde{x}_{it}$ is then orthogonal to the transformed error
$\tilde{\varepsilon}_{it}$, so OLS on @eq-app-within delivers a
consistent estimate of $\beta$. $\square$
:::

# References {.unnumbered}

::: {#refs}
:::
